perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all information provided by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party prior to or following the execution of this Agreement and in any way relating to the Purpose (including, without limitation, any and all intellectual property, business secrets, business inform ation, business plans, financial and pricing information, business practices, financial statements and reports, project specifications, projections, schematics and drawings, trade secrets, processes, materials, customer lists, supplier lists, sales volume, territories, markets, current, future or potential acquisitions, technical, production, operational, marketing or sales information disclosed hereunder) provided in connection therewith, regardless of form or format, provided however that Confidential Inf ormation shall not include information which the Receiving Party can establish through written records: (a) is, at the time of disclosure, readily available to the trade or the public through no fault of the Receiving Party , provided however that that where any part of such information is readily available to the trade or the public but a compilation of information which includes such part is not so available then such compilation shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder; or (b) was rightfully in its possession prior to the disclosure of such Confidential Information to the Receiving Party by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean information exchanged between the parties. provided however that Confidential Information shall not include information which the Receiving Party can establish through written records: (a) is, at the time of disclosure, readily available to the trade or the public through no fault of the Receiving Party , provided however that that where any part of such information is readily available to the trade or the public but a compilation of information which includes such part is not so available then such compilation shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder; or (b) was rightfully in its possession prior to the disclosure of such Confidential Information to the Receiving Party by, or on behalf of, the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed list of what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' including specific items like 'business secrets', 'financial and pricing information', 'customer lists', etc., and replacing it with a vague 'information exchanged between the parties', it creates uncertainty. Later sections rely on a clear definition. Specifically, Section 3 states 'The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information'. Without a precise definition, it's unclear exactly what interest the Receiving Party is obligated to protect, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers and employees ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Repre sentative failing to do so.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information,' while the changed text replaces this with a much broader and less specific definition: 'information exchanged between the parties.' This omission of specific categories (business secrets, customer lists, etc.) directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information'. The changed text makes it unclear what 'interest' needs to be protected, as it's no longer precisely defined what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' leading to a strong in-text contradiction. This adheres to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers and employees ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Repre sentative failing to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers and employees ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Repre sentative failing to do so.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential', in Section 3, while Section 9 states 'The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party ', it introduces an inconsistency. Section 3 no longer explicitly mandates keeping the information confidential. Section 9 still assumes a confidentiality obligation, creating an in-text contradiction. Even though Section 3 requires that the Representative treat the Confidetial Information as Confidential, the fact of not keeping the information confidential, impacts the representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information shall cause irreparable damage to the Disclosing Party . The Confidential Information is being disclosed by the Disclosing Party in specific reliance on the commitments and representations of the Receiving P arty in this Agreement. It is accordingly agreed that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specifically enforce the terms and provisions hereof, in addition to any other remedy to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential'. The changed text removes the phrase 'and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential'. The contradicted text states 'The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party'. By removing the obligation to 'keep the Confidential Information confidential', the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes that such obligation exists, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the provided explanation and fitting the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'as directed by the Disclosing Party', it contradicts section 7. Also by omitting the phrase 'and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information' it means that the Receiving Party, doesn't need to destroy any compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information. The act of returning the confidential information, when is finished, doesn't mean that the other part needs to destroy compilations, analysis or derivate works.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request return the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'as directed by the Disclosing Party' and 'and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information' from the original text. This creates a contradiction because the changed text now does not explicitly require the Receiving Party to destroy compilations, analyses, or derivative works related to the confidential information, which conflicts with the initial obligation outlined in the original text and, by extension, potentially any other section referencing a full and complete return or destruction of all confidential information and related materials. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, however the omitted text is what causes the contradiction to exist.

perturbation.original_text: All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party , and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party . The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party . The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party', while section 9, indicates that 'The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party', the omition in section 8, creates a contradiction. The Receiving Party now, is not obligated to hold in trust and confidence the information, but according to section 9, the nature of the information should be considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information shall cause irreparable damage to the Disclosing Party . The Confidential Information is being disclosed by the Disclosing Party in specific reliance on the commitments and representations of the Receiving P arty in this Agreement. It is accordingly agreed that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specifically enforce the terms and provisions hereof, in addition to any other remedy to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party.' This was removed in the changed text. The contradicted text states, 'The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party'. The omission in the changed text removes the explicit obligation for the Receiving Party to hold the information in trust and confidence, creating a strong contradiction with the contradicted text, which implies the information should be treated as such.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding during the Purpose and shall remain in effect for a period or 2 years after the Purpose is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding during the Purpose .
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'and shall remain in effect for a period or 2 years after the Purpose is completed', the agreement doesn't specify how long it will be effective. While Section 11, defines that the agreement is binding during the Purpose, it doesn't define what happen after it. This can create a contradiction, since Section 7, specifies that 'The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information', section 7 doesn't state any date for requesting the confidential information to be destroyed or returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the agreement's duration as during the Purpose and for 2 years after. The changed text removes the '2 years after' clause, creating an omission. Section 7 mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request but lacks a specific timeframe. The omission of the agreement's duration in the changed text creates a contradiction because it becomes unclear when the obligation to return or destroy the confidential information expires, directly conflicting with the indefinite obligation in Section 7. This absence of a defined end date introduces uncertainty and potential unenforceability, constituting a strong in-text contradiction.
